intro: The clearest split is that PubNub has a free tier while Pusher does not. Pusher is Real-time messaging platform with Channels for pub/sub and Beams for push notifications; PubNub is Real-time pub/sub messaging network with 800ms global delivery SLA and 70+ SDK support. The data points Pusher at chat applications and live collaboration features, and PubNub at chat applications and live collaboration features. Pricing: Pusher is seat; PubNub is subscription from $98/month. Lock-in is medium for Pusher and medium for PubNub, with DX at 5/5 vs 5/5 and transparency at 3/5 vs 4/5. On listed strengths, Pusher emphasises chat applications and live collaboration features, and PubNub emphasises chat applications and live collaboration features. Both offer an enterprise tier for larger rollouts. Honest trade-off: neither fits static projects, so if that is your context, look elsewhere; otherwise pick on the pivotal factor above and the DX score that matters most to your team.
quick_take: Pusher is for chat applications; PubNub is for chat applications; decide based on whether a free tier matters.
verdict_a: Choose Pusher if starting on a paid subscription upfront is acceptable and your workload matches chat applications. Skip it if your context is static projects.
verdict_b: Choose PubNub if you want to start on a free tier before paying and your workload matches chat applications. Skip it if your context is static projects.

faq.a: Entry pricing is listed as unknown for Pusher and $98/month for PubNub. PubNub offers a free tier while Pusher does not, so initial cost favors PubNub. Total cost at scale depends on usage and plan details that are not in this dataset, so confirm with each vendor.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Pusher to PubNub?
faq.a: Pusher is tagged with medium lock-in (proprietary pub/sub, swap to ably possible). PubNub is tagged with medium lock-in (subscription pubsub, client sdks portable). Plan for SDK replacement, data export, and a parallel-run period; the dataset does not include migration tooling details.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, Pusher or PubNub?
faq.a: Both are rated 5/5 for developer experience in this dataset. With equal scores, the choice comes down to the specific SDK surface and docs you will use day to day. Run a short spike on each before deciding.

faq.q: Is PubNub a good alternative to Pusher?
faq.a: Yes for teams whose workload matches chat applications, since both list the same fit. PubNub is flagged as not for static projects, so confirm your context does not fall there. Note that moving from Pusher to PubNub means gaining access to a free tier.
